In the run-up to Brexit, the uncertainty already caused a slowdown in trade between the United Kingdom (UK) and the EU. The consequences for Dutch exports were significant. This was partly the result of the malaise in the British car industry, which is still experiencing a lot of disruption from Brexit due to its interconnectedness with the car industry and suppliers on the European mainland.
